title: Fair Payment Terms
content: Client shall pay Consultant the fees set forth in Exhibit A within thirty (30) days of receipt of an accurate and undisputed invoice. In the event of a bona fide dispute regarding any invoice, Client shall pay the undisputed portion when due and the parties shall work in good faith to resolve the disputed portion within fifteen (15) business days. Late payments shall accrue simple interest at the lesser of 1% per month or the maximum rate permitted by applicable law.

title: Fair Termination Clause
content: Either party may terminate this Agreement (a) for material breach by the other party that remains uncured for thirty (30) days after written notice, or (b) upon sixty (60) days written notice for convenience. Upon termination, Consultant shall be paid for all work completed through the effective date of termination plus any non-cancellable expenses incurred. All provisions that by their nature should survive termination, including confidentiality, limitation of liability, and payment obligations, shall so survive.

title: Balanced Limitation of Liability
content: Neither party shall be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, or consequential damages arising out of or related to this Agreement, including lost profits, except in cases of gross negligence, willful misconduct, fraud, or breach of confidentiality obligations. Each party's total aggregate liability under this Agreement shall not exceed the greater of (a) the total fees paid or payable under this Agreement during the twelve (12) months preceding the claim, or (b) one hundred thousand dollars ($100,000). These limitations shall not apply to indemnification obligations, breach of confidentiality, or infringement of intellectual property rights.

title: Mutual Confidentiality Clause
content: Each party (the 'Receiving Party') agrees to hold in strict confidence all non-public information disclosed by the other party (the 'Disclosing Party'), whether in writing, orally, or through any other medium, that is clearly identified as confidential or that a reasonable person would understand to be confidential (collectively, 'Confidential Information'). The Receiving Party shall use at least the same degree of care as it uses with its own similar confidential information, but in no event less than reasonable care. Confidential Information excludes information that (a) is or becomes publicly available through no breach, (b) was lawfully known prior to disclosure, (c) is independently developed without use of Confidential Information, or (d) is required to be disclosed by law, provided the Receiving Party gives prompt notice and reasonable cooperation to limit such disclosure.

title: Balanced IP Ownership
content: Consultant retains all right, title, and interest in its pre-existing materials, tools, methodologies, and know-how ('Consultant IP'). Upon full payment, Consultant assigns to Client all right, title, and interest in the deliverables specifically created for Client under this Agreement ('Work Product'), excluding Consultant IP. Consultant grants Client a perpetual, irrevocable, royalty-free, worldwide license to use any Consultant IP incorporated into the Work Product solely to the extent necessary for Client to use the Work Product. Each party represents that its contributions will not infringe the intellectual property rights of any third party.

title: Fair Data Processing Clause
content: Each party shall comply with all applicable data protection laws, including the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA), as applicable. Consultant shall process personal data only on documented instructions from Client and shall implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect personal data against unauthorized access, alteration, disclosure, or destruction. Consultant shall notify Client of any data breach involving Client's personal data within forty-eight (48) hours of discovery. Consultant shall not sell, retain, or use Client's personal data for any purpose other than performing the Services under this Agreement.

title: Reasonable Non-Solicitation
content: During the term of this Agreement and for a period of twelve (12) months thereafter, each party agrees not to solicit for employment or hire any employee of the other party who was materially involved in the performance of this Agreement, provided that this restriction shall not apply to general public solicitations not targeted at such employees. The parties agree that this restriction is reasonable in scope and duration and is necessary to protect the legitimate business interests of each party. Nothing in this clause shall restrict a party from engaging in its ordinary course of business or serving any client or customer.

title: Fair Dispute Resolution
content: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to its conflict of laws principles. Before initiating any formal legal proceedings, the parties shall first attempt to resolve any dispute through good-faith negotiation for a period of thirty (30) days. If negotiation fails, the parties shall submit the dispute to non-binding mediation administered by a mutually agreed mediator, with costs shared equally. Any litigation shall be brought exclusively in the state or federal courts located in the agreed venue, and each party irrevocably consents to such jurisdiction and waives any objection to venue.

title: Fair Force Majeure Provision
content: Neither party shall be liable for any delay or failure to perform its obligations under this Agreement to the extent such delay or failure results from causes beyond its reasonable control, including acts of God, war, terrorism, civil unrest, epidemic, pandemic, fire, flood, earthquake, governmental action, or failure of utilities or telecommunications networks (each a 'Force Majeure Event'). The affected party shall (a) promptly notify the other party of the Force Majeure Event, its expected duration, and the obligations affected, and (b) use commercially reasonable efforts to mitigate the impact and resume performance. If a Force Majeure Event continues for more than thirty (30) days, either party may terminate this Agreement without liability upon written notice.

title: Balanced Warranty Terms
content: Consultant warrants that (a) the Services shall be performed in a professional and workmanlike manner consistent with industry standards, (b) Consultant has the full right and authority to enter into and perform this Agreement, and (c) the Work Product, when delivered, shall materially conform to the specifications set forth in the applicable statement of work. EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY STATED HEREIN, THE SERVICES AND WORK PRODUCT ARE PROVIDED 'AS IS,' AND CONSULTANT DISCLAIMS ALL IMPLIED WARRANTIES, INCLUDING MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. Client warrants that it has the authority to grant Consultant access to any Client systems or data necessary to perform the Services.

title: Fair Employee Notice Period
content: The Employee shall provide thirty (30) days written notice to the Employer prior to resignation. The Employer may, at its sole discretion, waive the notice period in whole or in part or provide pay in lieu of notice calculated at the Employee's base salary rate. During the notice period, the Employee shall continue to perform their duties and cooperate in the orderly transition of responsibilities. The Employer shall not withhold termination documents, relieving letters, or final settlement on account of the notice period if the Employee has completed at least fifteen (15) days of active service during the notice period.

title: Fair Employer Termination Procedure
content: The Employer may terminate this Agreement for cause immediately upon written notice in cases of gross misconduct, fraud, willful insubordination, or material breach of employment terms. For performance-related termination, the Employer shall (a) provide a written warning specifying the performance deficiencies, (b) allow the Employee a reasonable improvement period of not less than thirty (30) days, and (c) provide reasonable support and feedback during the improvement period. Termination without cause shall require thirty (30) days written notice or pay in lieu of notice. In all cases, the Employee shall receive a written statement of reasons for termination.

title: Fair Employee Transfer Policy
content: The Employer may transfer the Employee to an alternative location or role upon providing at least fifteen (15) days written notice. For transfers exceeding fifty (50) kilometers from the current work location, the Employer shall provide a reasonable relocation allowance covering moving expenses and temporary accommodation for up to seven (7) days. The Employee has the right to decline a transfer on valid grounds including proven medical hardship, caregiving responsibilities for immediate family members, or lack of comparable facilities at the new location for dependents with special needs. No adverse action shall be taken against an Employee who reasonably declines a transfer.

title: Fair Employee Gratuity Terms
content: The Employer shall pay gratuity to the Employee in accordance with the Payment of Gratuity Act, 1972, or applicable local law, whichever is more favorable to the Employee. Gratuity shall be calculated at fifteen (15) days wages for each completed year of service, based on the last drawn basic salary plus dearness allowance. Payment of gratuity shall be made within thirty (30) days of the Employee's last working day, regardless of the reason for separation. Gratuity payment shall not be conditioned upon the Employer receiving payment from any client, customer, or third party. Interest at 10% per annum shall accrue on delayed gratuity payments beyond the thirty-day period.

title: Fair Employee Confidentiality Clause
content: The Employee shall maintain the confidentiality of all proprietary and trade secret information of the Employer for a period of two (2) years following termination of employment. Confidential Information does not include information that (a) is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the Employee, (b) was known to the Employee prior to employment as evidenced by written records, or (c) the Employee is required to disclose by law, court order, or governmental authority. Nothing in this clause shall prevent the Employee from reporting suspected violations of law to any government agency, making protected whistleblower disclosures, or cooperating in any government investigation.

title: Fair Employee Non-Compete Clause
content: During employment and for a period of six (6) months following termination, the Employee shall not directly or indirectly engage in any business that is in direct competition with the Employer's core business within the same geographic territory where the Employee performed services. During the restricted period, the Employer shall pay the Employee compensation equal to fifty percent (50%) of the Employee's last drawn monthly base salary, paid monthly in arrears. The non-compete restriction shall apply only to competitors specifically identified in the employment letter, not to all businesses in the Employer's industry. This clause shall not restrict the Employee from accepting employment with a non-competitor or in a different business vertical.

title: Fair Employee IP Assignment
content: The Employee agrees to assign to the Employer all inventions, discoveries, improvements, and works of authorship created within the scope of employment and using the Employer's resources during working hours ('Work Product'). The Employee retains full ownership of any intellectual property (a) developed entirely on the Employee's own time, (b) without the use of the Employer's equipment, supplies, facilities, or trade secret information, and (c) that does not relate to the Employer's business or actual or anticipated research. The Employer shall not claim ownership over the Employee's pre-existing intellectual property unless such IP is specifically contributed to a project as documented in writing.

title: Fair Employee Indemnity Clause
content: The Employer agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the Employee against all claims, liabilities, losses, damages, and reasonable legal expenses arising from the Employee's performance of duties within the scope of employment, except where such claims result from the Employee's gross negligence, willful misconduct, or fraud. The Employee's obligation to indemnify the Employer is limited to cases of proven gross negligence or willful misconduct and shall not exceed the greater of (a) three (3) months of the Employee's base salary, or (b) the amount covered under the Employer's applicable insurance policy. The Employer shall maintain adequate directors and officers or professional liability insurance covering employees.

title: Fair Employee Salary Payment Terms
content: The Employer shall pay the Employee's monthly salary on or before the seventh (7th) day of each calendar month. Salary shall be paid via electronic bank transfer to the Employee's designated bank account. The Employer shall provide the Employee with a detailed written salary statement showing gross salary, all deductions (tax, provident fund, professional tax, insurance), and net salary paid. Any unauthorized deduction from salary is prohibited. In the event of salary payment delay exceeding seven (7) calendar days, simple interest at the rate of 1% per month shall accrue on the outstanding amount from the due date until payment is made. Interest on delayed salary shall be paid along with the next month's salary.

title: Fair Employee Working Hours Clause
content: The Employee's normal working hours shall not exceed forty-eight (48) hours per week, with a maximum of nine (9) hours per day exclusive of rest intervals. The Employee shall be entitled to a rest break of at least thirty (30) minutes after every five (5) hours of continuous work. Any work performed beyond the normal working hours shall be treated as overtime and compensated at twice (2x) the Employee's regular hourly rate. Overtime shall be voluntary and not mandatory. The Employee is entitled to one (1) full rest day per week, typically Sunday. The total overtime hours in any quarter shall not exceed one hundred twenty-five (125) hours. The Employer shall maintain accurate records of all hours worked and overtime paid.
